summary: Miller’s flawless sound‑board restoration yields a crystal‑clear, beefy audio picture of an iconic June 1973 show highlighted by monumental “Eyes of the World” and soaring “Dark Star.”
review: This recording captures one of the band’s most celebrated 1973 performances – the second set is repeatedly called a standout with towering jams on Eyes of the World, Dark Star, Bird Song and Here Comes Sunshine. Charlie Miller’s upgrade removes the usual board sterility, delivering a crisp, well‑balanced sound despite minor clipping, making the show as enjoyable to listen to now as it was live.

ai_rating.rationale: 16 of 19 reviewers gave five stars and praised both the performance and Miller’s restoration; only a few noted board sterility, so the overall assessment is strongly positive.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Beefy, slightly clipped source that Miller has electronically clarified; listeners describe it as crisp, clear and more listenable than any audience tape. A handful note a dry/sterile character but overall the mix is balanced with good instrument separation.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set is described as tight and reminiscent of 8/28/72, while the second set delivers the show’s high point with extended jams. The final set caps the night with a powerful Dickey Betts solo and classic closers.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s lead work is repeatedly praised – shredding, soaring solos on Dark Star, Eyes of the World and other jams; a few reviewers note a dip late in the set but overall his playing is called “shredding the time‑space continuum.”
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass is noted for solid presence and good separation, especially in the later reviews that highlight his “atonal edge” and steady groove.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s keyboards receive a brief nod for harmonic support and vocal transitions; no major criticisms are mentioned.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill (Billy) Kreutzmann’s drumming is highlighted as “jazz‑inflected,” keeping time impeccably and adding excitement to the jams.
